claim_boundary: This guard-model proof blocks only candidate admission when declared target-native obligation evidence is missing or native-failed. It does not independently detect the underlying physical, mapping, topology, workflow, or evidence defect and does not certify upstream truth.

depth_profile.claim_boundary: PhysicsGuard owns the family baseline semantics and every target-local current-model purpose, failure, native oracle, proof, residual risk, and bounded claim. SkillGuard only executes and reconciles the declared family baseline inventory; that receipt cannot close a current model.

functions.business_intent: Prove the maintained family baseline checker capability only: Prevent a test file from authorizing validation unless file/field identities, units, timing, testbench/model bindings, per-signal depth, mappings, and project gaps are complete and current.

action.summary: Validate the immutable PhysicsGuard family baseline contract; this is not current-model purpose authority.
Admit only the family baseline candidate bound to its exact baseline contract and ordered authoring chain.
Execute the mandatory family baseline known-good proof.
Prove the maintained family baseline blocks its declared regression case: Candidate is not proven against file or field identity is missing.
Prove the maintained family baseline blocks its declared regression case: Candidate is not proven against unit or timing contract mismatches.
Prove the maintained family baseline blocks its declared regression case: Candidate is not proven against testbench or model binding is wrong.
Prove the maintained family baseline blocks its declared regression case: Candidate is not proven against per-signal evidence is shallow.
Prove the maintained family baseline blocks its declared regression case: Candidate is not proven against project-level gap is hidden.
